name: Tomb of the Unknown Soldier
description: Single-clue route at the heart of modern Athens.

quiz.question: Where do the evzones guard the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier and the Eternal Flame? This square took its name from the Constitution that the first king of Greece was forced to grant here.

name: Old Town
description: Single-clue route in the oldest neighborhood of Athens.

quiz.question: The oldest continuously inhabited district of Athens, nestling under the Acropolis. Ottomans and locals left their mark on its narrow streets and neoclassical houses. Which neighborhood is this?

name: Temple of Olympian Zeus
description: Single-clue route at the largest temple of ancient Greece.

quiz.question: The largest temple in ancient Greece, begun in the 6th century BC and completed by the Roman emperor Hadrian. Which god was worshipped here?

name: Square and Bazaar
description: Single-clue route at the flea market and old monastery site.

quiz.question: The flea market and metro station bear the name of a small monastery that once stood here. Which district at the foot of the Acropolis is this?

name: Ancient Civic Heart
description: Single-clue route at the ancient marketplace and civic center.

quiz.question: Where did Socrates and the Athenians debate, trade, and run the city? The ancient marketplace and civic center of classical Athens.

name: Ancient Cemetery
description: Single-clue route at the ancient potters' quarter and cemetery.

quiz.question: This district took its name from the potters who worked here. It was also the main cemetery of ancient Athens, with the Sacred Gate leading to Eleusis. Which site is this?

name: Tower of the Winds
description: Single-clue route at the Roman Agora and the ancient clocktower.

quiz.question: In the Roman Agora stands an octagonal marble tower that once told the time with a water clock and a wind vane. What is it called?

name: Lysikrates Monument
description: Single-clue route at the first monument to be supported by Corinthian columns on the outside.

quiz.question: This circular monument in Plaka was built by a choregos to support the tripod he won in a drama contest. It gave its name to the street. Which monument is this?

name: Zappeion
description: Single-clue route at the neoclassical exhibition hall and gardens.

quiz.question: This building was donated by the Zappas family for the revival of the Olympic Games and now hosts exhibitions and summits. It stands in the National Garden. What is it called?

name: Panathenaic Stadium
description: Single-clue route at the marble stadium that hosted the first modern Olympics.

quiz.question: The only stadium in the world built entirely of marble. It hosted the Panathenaic Games in antiquity and the first modern Olympics in 1896. What is its popular name?

name: Syntagma to Plaka
description: Two-clue route from the modern heart of the city to the old town.

quiz.question: The heart of modern Athens, where the Constitution was proclaimed in the 19th century. Which square is this?
The oldest neighborhood of Athens, beneath the Acropolis, with Byzantine and Ottoman traces. Which area are you in?

name: Agora and Stoa of Attalos
description: Two-clue route in the Ancient Agora: civic center and museum.

quiz.question: Where did Socrates and the Athenians debate and run the city? The ancient marketplace and civic center.
This reconstructed building was a gift from a king of Pergamon. Which stoa houses the Agora Museum?

name: Hadrian to Zeus
description: Two-clue route from Hadrian's Gate to the Temple of Olympian Zeus.

quiz.question: The Roman emperor Hadrian built this gate to mark the boundary between old and new Athens. What is it called?
The largest temple in Greece, completed by Hadrian. Which god was honoured here?

name: Roman Agora to Tower of the Winds
description: Two-clue route through the Roman market and its famous clocktower.

quiz.question: Julius Caesar and Augustus funded this market square, separate from the older Greek Agora. What is it called?
Inside the Roman Agora, this tower once combined a water clock, sundials, and a wind vane. Name it.

name: Acropolis Three Landmarks
description: Three-clue route along the pedestrian street and the sacred rock.

quiz.question: You walk along the pedestrian street that offers the first clear view of the sacred rock. Which landmark stands above you?
Here the Parthenon was built for Athena. Which temple crowns the Acropolis?
The hill where the council of elders met and where Paul is said to have preached. Which rock is this?

name: Three Hills – Acropolis, Areopagus, Pnyx
description: Three-clue route linking the Acropolis with the hills of justice and democracy.

quiz.question: The sacred rock and the Parthenon. Which site was the religious and symbolic center of ancient Athens?
The hill of the ancient homicide court and the place where Paul addressed the Athenians. Which rock is this?
Where did the Athenian assembly meet to vote and debate? The birthplace of direct democracy.

name: From Temple of Zeus to Kallimarmaro
description: Three-clue route from ancient religion to the modern Olympic revival.

quiz.question: The largest temple in Greece, dedicated to the king of the gods. Hadrian completed it. Which temple is this?
The Zappas family built this neoclassical hall to revive the Olympic idea. It stands next to the National Garden. What is it?
The all-marble stadium where the first modern Olympics opened in 1896. Greeks call it Kallimarmaro. What is it?
